headline: Chips Lead a Narrow Monday Rally as Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on the Tape
body_html:
Monday's session closed with a cautiously positive tilt — 211 names advancing against 184 declining across a 396-stock universe — but the gains were far from evenly distributed. Semiconductors dominated the leaderboard, surging 2.39% as a group and dragging networking and storage names along for the ride. Industrials added a modest 0.85%, suggesting some appetite for hard-asset exposure, while Energy ended essentially flat at -0.06%.

The rotation story ran in the opposite direction for rate-sensitive and consumer-facing corners of the market. Homebuilders shed 1.26%, Materials dropped 1.06%, and Consumer names fell 0.75%, reflecting lingering unease over Middle East developments — including escalating rhetoric around Iran following Supreme Leader Khamenei's funeral — and a softening U.S. services-sector print for June. Healthcare slipped 0.29% as diagnostics names weighed on the group.

Credo Technology was the session's standout, vaulting nearly 10% to pace the semiconductor complex, while Arista Networks and Western Digital each posted gains north of 7%, reinforcing the AI-infrastructure theme that has propelled connectivity and storage hardware names throughout 2026. On the flip side, Solstice Advanced Materials cratered more than 15%, and the auto-parts retail duo of O'Reilly Automotive and AutoZone each shed roughly 6.5%, underscoring just how selective Monday's buying really was.

briefs.headline: Credo Technology Surges Nearly 10% to Pace the Semis Complex
briefs.body: Credo Technology finished Monday as the session's top performer, advancing 9.77% to lead a broad semiconductor rally. The connectivity-chip specialist has benefited from sustained capital spending on AI networking infrastructure, and Monday's move extended a theme that has made the semis group the clearest winner of 2026's first half.

briefs.headline: Solstice Advanced Materials Tumbles More Than 15% in Session's Ugliest Drop
briefs.body: Specialty-chemicals name Solstice Advanced Materials suffered the day's steepest decline, falling 15.14% with no immediate corporate announcement to explain the severity of the move. The selloff dragged the broader Materials sector down 1.06%, making it one of Monday's worst-performing groups alongside Homebuilders.

briefs.headline: Auto-Parts Retailers Hit Hard as Consumer Caution Spreads
briefs.body: O'Reilly Automotive and AutoZone each shed roughly 6.5% on Monday, pacing declines in the Consumer and retail sectors. The synchronized drop suggests macro-level concern about discretionary spending rather than a company-specific catalyst, arriving on the same day U.S. services-sector data showed growth cooling in June.

briefs.headline: Iran Uncertainty Lingers Over Markets After Khamenei Funeral
briefs.body: Geopolitical risk stayed elevated Monday as Khamenei's funeral drew crowds calling for retaliation against the United States, while President Trump warned that a deal with Tehran must materialize or the U.S. would 'finish the job.' An ECB official separately cautioned that the economic shock stemming from the Iran situation has not yet fully run its course.

briefs.headline: Suez Canal Route Reopens as Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd Resume Passage
briefs.body: Global shipping giants Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd announced a return to the Suez Canal trade route, a development that could meaningfully reduce transit times and freight costs for goods moving between Asia and Europe. The move marks a significant normalization of commercial shipping lanes that had been disrupted by Red Sea security concerns.

briefs.headline: NATO Defence Spending Commitments Climb as Dutch Announce Major Deals
briefs.body: The Netherlands said it would unveil more than three billion euros in defence contracts at an upcoming NATO forum, adding to a growing roster of European military-spending pledges. The announcement comes as allied nations convene to coordinate posture amid ongoing regional instability, with Sikorsky separately tapped to help fund a new White House helicopter landing facility.
